Description: Attend founder events, maintain relationships with operators, angels, and other VCs to build a steady stream of warm introductions to promising startups before they hit the market.
Output: New startup added to CRM or research list.

Description: Track new investments made by top-tier VCs (Benchmark, Sequoia, a16z, Founders Fund) to surface co-investment opportunities and identify hot companies early.
Output: New companies flagged and added to watchlist.

Description: Systematically review YC, Techstars, and other accelerator batch lists each cycle to identify promising companies that fit the fund's thesis.
Output: Shortlist of companies to screen added to research list.

Description: Monitor TechCrunch, LinkedIn, newsletters, and data aggregators for new companies announcing fundraises or milestones.
Output: New companies identified and logged in CRM.

Description: Proactively query databases for companies matching the fund's investment thesis by stage, sector, geography, and traction metrics.
Output: List of companies to evaluate added to deal flow.

Description: Email or call LPs, advisors, and portfolio CEOs to ask for introductions to founders building in priority areas.
Output: Warm introductions to founders received.

Description: Track technical founders shipping open source projects or consumer products in high-priority verticals to surface companies before they raise.
Output: Companies added to radar for outreach.

Description: Before attending a major conference (SaaStr, YC Demo Day, Founders Forum), identify which companies and founders to prioritize meeting.
Output: Meeting schedule built and calendar blocked.

Description: Review what competing funds have recently backed to understand market dynamics, avoid overlap, and spot signals of where top investors see opportunity.
Output: Market intelligence notes saved to CRM.

Description: Triage unsolicited pitch decks and intro requests from founders, syndicates, and scouts to determine which warrant further review.
Output: Pass decision or advancement to initial screen.

Description: Review basic details on a company—team background, market, product, stage, and traction—to decide whether it fits the investment thesis and warrants deeper attention.
Output: Pass or advance to outreach; CRM updated.

Description: Evaluate the startup's pitch materials for narrative clarity, market opportunity, business model, team quality, traction, and fundraising ask.
Output: Decision to advance or pass; key questions noted.

Description: Assess Total Addressable Market (TAM), Serviceable Addressable Market (SAM), and Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM) to determine if the opportunity can support a venture-scale outcome.
Output: Market size assessment saved to startup record.

Description: Evaluate whether the founding team has deep domain expertise, lived experience, or an unfair insight advantage in the problem they are solving.
Output: Founder assessment notes added to CRM.

Task: Check stage and check size fit
Description: Confirm the startup's current stage and fundraising ask align with the fund's check size, ownership targets, and stage strategy.
Output: Stage and fit confirmation logged.

Description: Use the fund's internal scoring rubric—covering founder quality, market size, product, and traction—to rank and compare the opportunity against others in the pipeline.
Output: Scored startup record with rank and flag.

Description: Review prior rounds, SAFEs, convertible notes, valuations, and existing investor names to understand the company's capitalization history.
Output: Funding history logged and cap table previewed.

Description: Evaluate whether the company's headquarters and target market align with the fund's geographic focus and ability to add value.
Output: Location and market fit assessment logged.

Description: Scan for press coverage, product launches, executive hires, and recent announcements to surface signals of momentum or risk.
Output: Signal notes added to startup record.

Description: Identify key direct and adjacent competitors, assess market positioning, and determine the startup's differentiation and defensibility.
Output: Competitive landscape notes saved.

Task: Add a startup to the CRM
Description: Log a new company's details—name, website, stage, sector, founder names, and source—into the fund's deal tracking system.
Output: CRM entry created and startup appears in deal flow.

Description: Move a company to the appropriate deal flow stage (Uncontacted, Outbound, First Call, Engage, Term Sheet) as the relationship progresses.
Output: Status updated and movement recorded in CRM.

Description: Document specific strengths and risks identified during initial review—such as a repeat founder, high churn, or category leadership—as structured notes on the startup.
Output: Highlights attached to the startup record.

Description: Craft and send a personalized introductory message to a founder that explains genuine investment interest and a specific reason for reaching out.
Output: Email or LinkedIn message sent; outreach logged.

Description: Identify a mutual connection and ask them to facilitate a warm introduction to a specific founder to increase response rate.
Output: Introduction request sent; awaiting intro.

Description: Re-engage founders who haven't responded to the initial outreach with a brief, non-pushy follow-up message.
Output: Follow-up message sent; outreach record updated.

Description: Coordinate calendars with the founder and confirm a 30-60 minute introductory call.
Output: Meeting booked and calendar invite sent.

Description: Send a brief thank-you note after the initial call with a summary of interest and clear next steps.
Output: Email sent; CRM updated with meeting notes.

Description: Contact a founder you have been tracking to check on fundraising progress, key milestones hit, and round status.
Output: Status update received and CRM refreshed.

Description: Have an initial introductory meeting with founders to hear the pitch, ask clarifying questions, and assess the team's vision, coachability, and communication style.
Output: Meeting notes logged; decision to advance or pass.

Description: Ask founders to walk through or provide access to the product to evaluate quality, user experience, and technical sophistication firsthand.
Output: Demo completed; product assessment notes saved.

Description: Observe how co-founders interact during calls and presentations to assess complementarity, alignment, and communication effectiveness.
Output: Team dynamics assessment logged.

Task: Log meeting notes and action items
Description: Record key takeaways, open questions, and agreed-upon next steps after every founder interaction to maintain an accurate relationship history.
Output: CRM updated with notes and next steps.

Task: Conduct a second or third founder meeting
Description: Hold deeper follow-up calls to explore product, go-to-market strategy, financials, and competitive positioning in greater detail.
Output: Deeper diligence notes; partner introduction scheduled.

Description: Arrange for a fund partner to meet the founders as a step toward investment committee review.
Output: Partner meeting scheduled.

Description: Ask the founding team to share financial documents, legal materials, metrics, and other diligence materials via a secure data room.
Output: Data room access granted.

Description: Analyze the startup's revenue model, cost structure, growth assumptions, and scenario forecasts to assess financial rigor and trajectory.
Output: Financial model assessment saved.

Description: Assess LTV, CAC, payback period, gross margins, and contribution margins to determine whether the business model is fundamentally healthy.
Output: Unit economics analysis documented.

Description: Analyze ARR growth, net revenue retention, churn rate, customer concentration, and contract terms to assess the durability of the revenue base.
Output: Revenue quality assessment logged.

Description: Review the company's monthly cash burn, cash on hand, and projected runway to understand urgency and capital efficiency.
Output: Burn and runway assessment saved.

Description: Speak directly with 3-5 existing customers to validate retention, satisfaction, product-market fit, and use cases.
Output: Reference call notes saved; key themes documented.

Task: Interview churned customers or lost prospects
Description: Reach out to customers who left or prospects who did not convert to understand competitive losses and product gaps.
Output: Risk identification notes added to diligence file.

Description: Assess the product's technical architecture, scalability, security posture, technical debt, and defensibility by engaging a technical expert or reviewing code.
Output: Technical DD report completed.

Description: Review corporate structure, IP ownership, material contracts, employment agreements, regulatory compliance, and pending litigation.
Output: Legal DD report completed; red flags flagged.

Description: Analyze ownership structure, existing investor stakes, option pool size, SAFE notes, and dilution implications for the proposed round.
Output: Cap table analysis and pro forma ownership completed.

Description: Confirm IP ownership, patent filings, trade secret protections, and whether core technology is defensible and owned by the company.
Output: IP assessment documented.

Description: Validate market size claims and dynamics using third-party industry reports, primary interviews, and competitive intelligence tools.
Output: Market validation report completed.

Description: Analyze historical P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow statements to verify the accuracy of reported financials.
Output: Financial statement review completed.

Description: Review customer cohorts by acquisition month to understand retention curves, monetization trends, and whether engagement is improving over time.
Output: Cohort analysis report saved.

Description: Analyze month-over-month and year-over-year growth rates, pipeline velocity, and leading indicators to assess momentum.
Output: Growth metrics report completed.

Description: Verify founder backgrounds, prior company histories, criminal records, and professional reputation through formal screening services.
Output: Background check report completed.

Description: Speak with prior employers, investors, colleagues, and employees who have worked closely with the founders to assess character, leadership, and track record.
Output: Reference notes summarized and saved.

Description: Assess the strength and durability of the startup's competitive advantage—network effects, switching costs, data flywheel, proprietary technology, or regulatory barrier.
Output: Competitive moat analysis documented.

Description: Evaluate compliance requirements, licensing obligations, and government or legal headwinds that could constrain the business.
Output: Regulatory risk assessment saved.

Description: Compare the startup's metrics to public company comps and recent private market transactions to calibrate valuation and performance.
Output: Benchmarking analysis completed.

Description: Assess whether the startup has achieved true PMF by reviewing retention cohorts, NPS, qualitative customer feedback, and organic growth signals.
Output: PMF assessment documented.

Description: Review the startup's technology choices—languages, infrastructure, tooling—for scalability, security, and team-market fit.
Output: Tech stack assessment saved.

Description: Systematically check that all required diligence documents are present and organized before finalizing the investment decision.
Output: Data room checklist completed; gaps flagged.

Description: Write a structured internal document summarizing the investment thesis, key risks, competitive dynamics, team assessment, and a formal invest or pass recommendation.
Output: Investment memo completed and shared with partners.

Description: Present findings and a formal recommendation to the fund's general partners or investment committee for approval to proceed.
Output: IC decision made; term sheet authorized or deal passed.

Description: Assess an appropriate entry valuation using comparable transactions, revenue multiples, DCF, and market-based benchmarks.
Output: Valuation range and recommendation documented.

Description: Discuss and negotiate economic terms (valuation, pro rata rights, option pool) and governance terms (board seat, information rights) with the founding team.
Output: Signed term sheet executed.

Description: Identify, vet, and bring in co-investors to fill the remainder of the round, ensuring cap table quality and strategic value.
Output: Round fully subscribed with co-investors committed.

Description: Evaluate whether the fund has conviction to lead the round (set terms, take board seat) or participate as a strategic follow-on investor.
Output: Investment role determined and communicated to founders.

Description: Work with legal counsel to finalize remaining diligence items between term sheet signing and close of the financing.
Output: All diligence items resolved; documents ready for execution.

Description: Engage legal counsel to review the Stock Purchase Agreement, Investor Rights Agreement, Right of First Refusal, and Co-Sale Agreement.
Output: Executed legal documents signed by all parties.

Task: Finalize cap table and wire capital
Description: Confirm the post-close cap table, option pool, and ownership percentages, then wire the investment amount to the company.
Output: Capital deployed and company on-boarded to portfolio.

Description: Coordinate with the portfolio company on the timing and content of a public announcement of the investment via press release or social media.
Output: Announcement published; deal visibility increased.

Description: Participate in monthly or quarterly board meetings to provide oversight, governance, and strategic input to the portfolio company.
Output: Board meeting notes and action items recorded.

Description: Review board materials, financial updates, and key metrics ahead of each board meeting and formulate questions and recommendations.
Output: Board prep notes completed; agenda reviewed.

Description: Advise portfolio CEOs on company strategy, pricing decisions, go-to-market approach, organizational design, or product direction.
Output: Strategic input delivered; decision supported.

Description: Assist a portfolio company in defining the role, sourcing candidates through the fund's network, and evaluating finalists for key executive hires.
Output: Hire made or shortlist of candidates provided.

Description: Connect a portfolio company with potential customers from the fund's LP base, co-investor networks, or the GP's personal network.
Output: Introduction sent; meeting facilitated.

Description: Connect a portfolio company with potential technology partners, distribution channels, advisors, or vendors who can accelerate growth.
Output: Introduction sent; partnership conversation initiated.

Description: Review monthly or quarterly financial and operational metrics for each portfolio company to track performance against plan.
Output: Updated KPI dashboard; issues flagged proactively.

Description: Assess the portfolio company's progress, current valuation, and terms to decide whether to invest in the next funding round.
Output: Follow-on investment decision made.

Description: Help an underperforming or capital-constrained portfolio company raise a bridge round or extend runway to reach the next milestone.
Output: Bridge round completed or plan formalized.

Description: Help a portfolio company evaluate acquisition offers, prepare for a sale process, or negotiate deal terms.
Output: M&A outcome achieved (acquisition close or decision to continue operating).

Description: Document key observations, decisions made, and action items from board meetings in which the fund participates as an observer rather than a board member.
Output: Observer notes saved and shared with relevant parties.

Description: Monitor portfolio companies for key milestones—product launches, fundraising announcements, major hires, and press coverage—to stay informed between board meetings.
Output: News feed and milestone log updated.

Description: Proactively identify early warning signs—high churn, cash crunch, team conflict, or lost market position—and develop a remediation plan.
Output: Remediation plan communicated to founders.

Description: Evaluate whether a portfolio company is ready for an IPO or M&A process by reviewing metrics, governance, and market conditions.
Output: Exit readiness assessment and timeline recommendation.

Description: Analyze high-value investments made by top-tier VCs in companies that were previously evaluated and passed on, to understand miss patterns.
Output: Missed deal logged; pass rationale reviewed and categorized.

Description: Write a comprehensive quarterly update to limited partners covering portfolio performance, new investments, exits, and fund-level metrics.
Output: LP report delivered on time.

Description: Notify LPs of capital deployment events and request the draw of committed capital according to the fund's drawdown schedule.
Output: Capital received from LPs and available for deployment.

Description: Host a formal annual meeting with LPs to present the fund's investment activity, portfolio status, financial performance, and market outlook.
Output: LP meeting completed; LP confidence maintained.

Description: Proactively maintain relationships with existing limited partners through updates, events, and introductions to portfolio companies.
Output: LP retention and satisfaction sustained.

Description: Monitor management fees, carry calculations, fund NAV, IRR, TVPI, and RVPI to maintain an accurate view of the fund's financial health.
Output: Fund performance report updated.

Task: Build and maintain an advisor network
Description: Identify and engage technical, operational, and domain experts who can help the fund evaluate deals and support portfolio companies.
Output: Advisor network expanded; experts matched to portfolio companies.

Task: Track industry trends and thesis development
Description: Monitor macro trends, emerging technologies, regulatory shifts, and category dynamics in priority sectors to sharpen the fund's investment thesis.
Output: Thesis updated; new conviction areas documented.

Description: Participate in key events (SaaStr, YC Demo Day, Web Summit) to source deals, meet founders, deepen relationships with co-investors, and build the fund's brand.
Output: New contacts added; potential deals identified.

Description: Proactively maintain working relationships with VCs who co-invest alongside the fund or refer proprietary deals.
Output: Co-investor referral network strengthened.

Description: Systematically map out the competitive landscape for a specific sector or thesis area to identify white spaces and the strongest companies to back.
Output: Market map completed and used to prioritize outreach.

Description: Write blog posts, give conference talks, or post insights on LinkedIn to build the fund's brand and attract high-quality inbound deal flow.
Output: Content published; brand visibility increased.

Description: Build a curated list of companies in a specific sector, cohort (e.g. YC W25), or theme for systematic coverage and comparison.
Output: Research list created and populated in the platform.

Description: Upload a CSV of companies to the CRM for batch processing, scoring, and assignment.
Output: Companies imported and available in deal flow pipeline.

Description: Set up or update the fund's internal scoring criteria for evaluating startups by sector, stage, and founder characteristics.
Output: Scoring rules updated; new companies auto-scored.

Description: Export CRM, portfolio, or pipeline data for reporting, financial modeling, or external analysis.
Output: Data export completed and shared or imported into analysis tool.

Task: Conduct reference check on a VC co-investor
Description: Before partnering with another fund on a deal, speak with founders or other GPs who have worked with them to assess value-add and co-investor quality.
Output: Co-investor quality assessment completed.

Description: Identify situations where a struggling portfolio company or target startup could be acquired for its team by a strategic buyer.
Output: Acqui-hire assessment and introductions made.

Task: Review and improve the fund's investment process
Description: Regularly audit the fund's sourcing, diligence, and portfolio management workflows to identify inefficiencies and best practices.
Output: Process improvements documented and implemented.
